scenario: Aurora Bank schedules SLA escalations for 6000 incidents per day in Camunda 8.8. A modeler uses an expression on an intermediate timer catch event so the wait duration is computed from a process variable remainingTime, and the team wants to confirm when the expression is evaluated and what data type the result must have.
question: When is the timer expression on this intermediate catch event evaluated and what must the result be?

options.text: It is evaluated on activating the timer catch event and the result must be a string in ISO 8601 format or an equivalent temporal value such as a date-time, duration, or cycle
It is evaluated on deploying the process definition and the result must be a number representing the number of milliseconds to wait before the timer fires at runtime
It is evaluated on activating the timer catch event and the result must be a number representing the wait duration in seconds rather than a string in ISO 8601 format
It is evaluated on receiving the first job by an external worker and the result must be a list of ISO 8601 strings to represent multiple alternative wait windows

a.text: Correct. For timer events other than timer start events, the timer expression is evaluated on activating the catch event. The result must be a string in the same ISO 8601 format as a static value, or an equivalent temporal value such as a date-time, duration, or cycle.

b.text: Incorrect. Deploy-time evaluation applies only when the timer expression belongs to a timer start event of the process; for an intermediate catch event the expression is evaluated on activation.

c.text: Incorrect. A numeric seconds value is not a valid result; the evaluation must produce an ISO 8601 string or an equivalent temporal value such as a date-time, duration, or cycle.

d.text: Incorrect. The expression is evaluated by the engine on activation of the catch event, not by an external worker, and the result must be a single temporal value rather than a list of alternatives.

explanation: A timer expression that belongs to a timer start event is evaluated on deployment, otherwise it is evaluated on activating the timer catch event. The result must be an ISO 8601 string or an equivalent temporal value such as a date-time, duration, or cycle.
